metadata.key_format: (fixture, claim_id, term, reference_form) tuple — parity with TW schema v11. ADR-107 tuple dedup is handled inside the walker, not here.

categories.description: Real drafting error in the source patent — walker is correctly flagging. MUST preserve (protect: true).
Reference uses the TC-plural prefix 该等/该些 which is invalid in CN drafting per CNIPA审查指南 (use 所述). The walker emits category:tw_contamination on the Q1 path and bypasses normal resolution (ADR-103).
Cannot be categorized from the excerpt alone. Requires reading the full claim text to disambiguate.
Stage 3 seed value for unaudited findings. Every real-corpus finding starts here. Stage 4 tuning rounds drain this bucket as each root cause earns its category.

Walker captured a term with trailing verb/adverb/particle that shouldn't be part of the noun phrase. R8–R12 trailing-strip work targets this shape. Residual entries here are the long-tail of sub-patterns not yet covered by the denylists.

Walker extended the capture past a legitimate noun boundary, typically into a fresh verb phrase or clause. Analogous to TW's walker_bug.noun_boundary_overgreedy. Fix shape is right-boundary lookahead or interior-verb denylist extension.

Walker captured a fragment that isn't a valid noun phrase — typically <2 CJK chars plus a particle, or a verb-phrase fragment. R5 single-char suppression targets part of this; residual is a heterogeneous tail.
Term is just 第N with no noun attached. R11 bare-ordinal suppression should have caught these; residuals are formula-ordinal variants or cross-boundary captures.

Term is cleanly introduced in a NON-ancestor claim (typically a sibling in method-vs-apparatus or independent-claim split). The walker's per-claim ancestor-chain design cannot resolve these without architectural change (Option B / Phase 9 #54). Documented known limitation; excluded from FP-rate denominator.

Term IS introduced cleanly in the ancestor chain, but the walker's intro-pattern set does not register it. Each coverage gap eventually earns its own F-family or denylist entry through a walker-round. Active entries here are the work remaining before close-out.

Intro site has `X和Y` / `X与Y` / `X及Y` shape; walker registered the whole string as a single intro rather than splitting on the conjunction. Downstream references to just X or just Y fail to resolve. R14f target.

metadata.notes: | Schema v11 triage (2026-04-16): auto-classified 423 active labels from 'unclassified' into walker_fp / miss_intro / ambig categories. Active-pool composition now: ambig=41 miss_intro.coverage_gap=146 tw_contamination=3 walker_fp.cross_branch_sibling=169 walker_fp.fragment=6 walker_fp.over_capture=59 walker_fp.trailing_residue=2. Classifier: shape heuristics + ancestor-chain search + trim-strip fallback. The 41 'ambig' entries are residual; cross_branch_sibling is Option B territory (Phase 9 #54). Categories walker_fp.* / miss_intro.* added to metadata for ADR-110 validation.

review_log.rationale: Sonnet ensemble correctly identified intros walker missed (verb-object bare nouns, Pattern B enumerator-form members, parent-claim bare-noun intros, compound-noun tokenization fragments). 2 cases retained as legit_drafting_error per Christopher's review.
